windows-gfx1151.blocked_reason: fork @ 09612d3c. MultiStat histogram CRASH and post-split index mismatch BOTH FIXED. (1) Histogram grid div-by-zero (numBlocks product, guarded ScaleBlockCountToOccupancy at 16 sites). (2) Post-split index mismatch = rocPRIM DeviceScan temp MISALIGNMENT in SortWithoutCub (split_points.cu): the scan working temp was sub-allocated at offset 4*part.Size (4-byte aligned) inside a shared buffer; rocPRIM needs it aligned, else the scan corrupts non-deterministically at tile boundaries -> wrong split indices. Fixed by 256-aligning the scan-temp offset (USE_HIP only). gfx1151 GPU-validated: cuda_util-ut 48/48, gpu_data-ut 20/20, methods-ut histograms 8/8 + exact-leaves 15/15 + multistat 5/6 (WithoutOneHot1/2/17 + WithOneHot3/13 pass; FatSplitPropsTest 2M re-running to confirm, uses radix path + just slow on APU); targets-ut TCombinationTargetTests 3/3 (dcg.cu) + Huber/MultiLogit/Tweedie pass. ONLY remaining failure: TAddingLangevinNoiseTest -- proven NOT a ROCm defect (GPU NextNormal bit-identical to host; pre-existing upstream test-design, won't fix). So GPU correctness is essentially fully validated. Pending: FatSplitPropsTest confirmation; full-app e2e not run. See notes.md 2026-06-02.
